Essential inorganic nutrients

Think about which kinds of molecules include Nitrogen and/or Phosphorus

Ion uptake – passive transport (channels) or co-transport
K + , Ca 2+ , Mg 2+
H 2 PO 4 -

Air is > 70% N 2 gas
N 2 ≠ usable nitrogen for plants
Nitrogen fixation by soil bacteria:
N 2   NH 3 or NH 4 +
Nitrification by soil bacteria:
NH 3 or NH 4 +   NO 2 -   NO 3 -

Legumes signal to Rhizobium bacteria in soil
Rhizobium cells signal back to plant
Only legumes can respond to signal from bacteria
Colonization of root begins
Plant invites bacteria into root
Symbiosis - Rhizobium bacteria live inside plant cells

nitrates to rest of plant
via xylem
Only legumes can
do this
Carnivorous plants live in nitrogen-poor soil
Fungal symbiosis – most plants do this

Mycorrhizae = roots + fungus
Ectomycorrhizae – fungal hyphae are outside plant cells
outer surface of root
in cortex, between cells
Endomycorrhizae – fungal hyphae inside root cells

Endomycorrhizae
